Cassava.note: Garri white used as price proxy. Farmgate ~45% below market due to middleman margin.

seasonNarrative.lean: Jan–Mar prices drop as stockpiled roots from the prior harvest reach markets.
seasonNarrative.peak: Oct–Nov prices rise as dry-season demand outpaces supply from the long-season crop.

Maize.note: Two seasons possible. Season 1 (Mar–Apr plant) typically yields higher prices at harvest.

seasonNarrative.lean: May–Jul prices drop as the main-season harvest floods the market.
seasonNarrative.peak: Sep–Nov prices recover as off-season stocks run low before the second harvest.

Plantain.note: SW Nigeria has the highest national plantain prices. Aug–Oct is the peak window.

seasonNarrative.lean: Feb–Apr prices fall as the long-season crop peaks in SW markets.
seasonNarrative.peak: Aug–Oct prices spike — lean-season supply shortage drives Sallah demand.

Tomato.note: Highly volatile. Jun-24 reached ₦3,262/kg — a real lean-season spike. Build this risk into your timing.

seasonNarrative.lean: May–Jul prices can spike or crash violently as dry-season supply collapses.
seasonNarrative.peak: Dec–Feb prices are typically the most stable, driven by harmattan demand.

Yam.note: New Yam Festival (Aug–Sep) drives price peaks. Aug–Nov is historically strong.

seasonNarrative.lean: Apr–Jun prices are suppressed as stored yams from the prior season meet early new yam.
seasonNarrative.peak: Aug–Oct prices peak around New Yam Festival demand.
